channel.title: Defense
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Senate threatens to freeze Hegseth’s travel in bid for boat strike videos, Iran school strike probe
item.description: Major defense legislation approved by the Senate Armed Services Committee freezes three quarters of the Defense Secretary’s travel budget until Congress gets what it wants.

media:content.media:title: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, right, talks with Senate Armed Services Committee Chair Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) following the committee's hearing on the Department of Defense budget, on Capitol Hill, in Washington, Thursday, April 30, 2026. (AP Photo/Cliff Owen)

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III

item.title: Hegseth's timeline for Strait of Hormuz is a bit murky
item.description: The Secretary of Defense initially stated that the waterway would reopen "immediately" after a deal was signed, only to clarify his comment moments later.

media:content.media:title: "Ultimately the blockade will stay as the strait opens. And then the blockade will open and then the straits open," Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth said.

item.dc:creator: By Cheyanne M. Daniels

item.title: Ukraine is winning the drone war. Now it needs to win over Trump.
item.description: Battlefield gains and drone deals with Europe have given Kyiv rare leverage heading into the G7 world leaders’ summit, even as Washington's attention is elsewhere.

media:content.media:title: Ukrainian troops load their comrade into a land combat drone during a training in Kharkiv region, Ukraine, on June 10, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Felicia Schwartz, Megan Messerly and Nicholas Vinocur
item.dc:contributor: By Felicia Schwartz, Megan Messerly and Nicholas Vinocur

item.title: Trump’s missile diplomacy strategy backfires
item.description: “You’re bombing what to get what at this point?” said a former Trump administration official.

media:content.media:title: A Hezbollah supporter chants slogans while holding an Iranian flag during a gathering against the U.S.-Israeli military operation with Iran in Dahiyeh, Lebanon on June 10, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary, Nahal Toosi and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary, Nahal Toosi and Jack Detsch

item.title: Pentagon resumes normal operations after lockdown
item.description: The Hazardous Materials Team from the Arlington, Virginia, Fire Department was on the scene to assist Pentagon officials, the department wrote on X.

media:content.media:title: The Pentagon is seen on May 19, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Gregory Svirnovskiy and Daniel Lippman
item.dc:contributor: By Gregory Svirnovskiy and Daniel Lippman

item.title: Pentagon locks down over ‘air quality issue’
item.description: The Hazardous Materials Team from the Arlington, Virginia, Fire Department was on the scene to assist Pentagon officials, the department wrote on X.

media:content.media:title: The Pentagon is seen on May 19, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Gregory Svirnovskiy and Daniel Lippman
item.dc:contributor: By Gregory Svirnovskiy and Daniel Lippman

item.title: Trump says US will resume 'attacking' Iran as negotiations stall
item.description: “They’ve taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them, now they will have to pay the price!!!” Trump posted to social media Wednesday.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump watches the start of Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden on June 8, 2026, in New York City.

item.dc:creator: By Cheyanne M. Daniels

item.title: After Trump's Director of National Intelligence pick, knives are out for the whole office
item.description: The nomination of housing official Bill Pulte has stirred wider discussions about trimming the office back — or getting rid of it.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump told The Wall Street Journal last week he’d told Bill Pulte the ODNI was "unnecessary and/or too big.”

item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow, John Sakellariadis and Gregory Svirnovskiy
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow, John Sakellariadis and Gregory Svirnovskiy

item.title: More women, Democratic veterans running for Congress this year
item.description: The number of veterans running for federal office this cycle is up 47 percent from 2024.

media:content.media:title: Reps. Marianette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa), center, and Jennifer Kiggans (R-Va.), right, are two of the 95 women veterans running for federal office this election cycle.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III

item.title: NASA quietly talking to Congress about more moon money
item.description: The agency has begun reaching out to lawmakers as it pushes to meet President Trump’s moon landing timeline.

media:content.media:title: The lunar surface is seen during the Artemis II lunar flyby, captured on April 6.

item.dc:creator: By Audrey Decker

item.title: Pentagon walks back Latter-day Saints Church classification after pushback from Utah lawmakers
item.description: In an update to faith codes, the Pentagon had classified the religion as separate from Christianity.

media:content.media:title: The temple of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints in Kensington, Maryland is pictured on April 18, 2022.

item.dc:creator: By Cheyanne M. Daniels

item.title: House GOP endorses Trump's Department of War renaming
item.description: The move by the House Armed Services Committee injects another partisan wedge issue into an already fraught defense bill.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Hegseth took on the title secretary of war last year, but the Pentagon's legal name remains the Department of Defense until Congress signs off.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Ukraine aid package passes House, bucking GOP leaders and Trump
item.description: Eighteen Republicans broke ranks to help pass the military assistance and Russia sanctions.

media:content.media:title: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) speaks with reporters outside the U.S. Capitol on April 19, 2023.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Pentagon likely to cancel missile deal with Germany over fears of Russia
item.description: The decision is part of a broader American retrenchment from the NATO alliance.

media:content.media:title: Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Thomas Hudner fires a Tomahawk missile at sea on March 1, 2026 during the Iran war.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary, Stefanie Bolzen and Chris Lunday
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary, Stefanie Bolzen and Chris Lunday

item.title: House panel demands more information on military firings
item.description: The move comes after the dismissal of multiple uniformed military leaders since President Donald Trump took office.

media:content.media:title: The provision represents a rebuke of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s personnel moves.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Iran vote caps Trump’s congressional losing streak
item.description: The House vote to halt the Iran war, while largely symbolic, further stymies the administration's political priorities during a week where the GOP has already scuttled several of the president’s goals.

media:content.media:title: A worker raises a pole bearing the Iranian flag at Enghelab Square in Tehran on June 3, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III

item.title: Trump’s intel pick delights MAGA and shocks nation’s spies
item.description: Bill Pulte, the head of a housing agency, is set to take the reins as the Director of National Intelligence — raising alarms on the Hill even as it delights the MAGA faithful.

media:content.media:title: Bill Pulte speaks with reporters at the White House on Jan. 9, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Asia reckons with China as Trump pulls back
item.description: America’s transactional approach to Europe is carrying over to Asia.

media:content.media:title: “The era of the United States subsidizing the defense of wealthy nations is over,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch

item.title: Hegseth tempers China criticism at Asia forum
item.description: The Pentagon chief, in a rare omission, avoided any mention of Taiwan in a speech to Asian officials.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth delivers his address during the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia's annual defense and security forum, in Singapore on May 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Paul McLeary

item.title: Trump’s moon mission at risk after huge rocket explosion
item.description: The destruction of a Blue Origin launchpad could thwart key goals of the president’s.

media:content.media:title: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket stands ready for launch at the Cape Canaveral Space Force station in Cape Canaveral, Florida, on April 18, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Audrey Decker

item.title: Pentagon puts building blocks in place for Cuba invasion
item.description: The Navy’s presence in the Caribbean has not reduced despite the Iran war.

media:content.media:title: A worker points to the U.S. Navy aircraft carrier USS Nimitz docking in the Gulf of Panama, March 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Trump faces first big loss on Iran war
item.description: Votes in the House and Senate this week to halt the military campaign would prove a stunning embarrassment for the president.

media:content.media:title: Pro-government demonstrators chant slogans as they wave Iranian flags in Tehran, Iran, on May 19, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Leo Shane III

item.title: Trump says he’ll talk to Taiwan’s president amid arms deal negotiations
item.description: Proposed direct contact between the U.S. and Taiwanese president overturns decades of diplomatic practice governing ties between the two countries.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump talks to reporters before boarding Air Force One on May 20, 2026, at Joint Base Andrews, Maryland.

item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Gregory Svirnovskiy
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Gregory Svirnovskiy

item.title: Senate advances anti-Iran war measure in surprise blow to Trump
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who lost his primary this weekend, became the latest GOP defector as critics took advantage of Republican absences to advance a war powers resolution.

media:content.media:title: War opponents notably gained another GOP defector in Sen. Bill Cassidy.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Hegseth enters the war between Donald Trump and Thomas Massie
item.description: The Defense secretary’s remarks are highly unusual for a civilian military leader.

media:content.media:title: Ed Gallrein, Kentucky Republican congressional candidate, greets Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on stage during an America First Workers Special Event in Hebron, Kentucky, on May 18, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Lisa Kashinsky
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Lisa Kashinsky

item.title: 4 crew members eject safely after 2 Navy jets crash during air show in Idaho
item.description: “Everyone is safe and I think that’s the most important thing,” a spokespersons said.

media:content.media:credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: A U.S. Navy EA18-G Growler is seen Dec. 17, 2025, over Ceiba, Puerto Rico.

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: USS Ford returns home after 11-month deployment supporting the Iran war and Maduro’s capture
item.description: The warship and two destroyers docked in Norfolk, Virginia, with about 5,000 sailors waiting to see their families for the first time since June.

media:content.media:title: A sailor is greeted after disembarking aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford at Naval Station Norfolk on May 16, 2026, in Norfolk, Virginia. The carrier returned home after an 11-month deployment, the longest since the Vietnam War. (Photo by Mike Kropf/Getty Images)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: ‘Slap in the face': Republicans skewer Pentagon over Poland move
item.description: Army leaders also appeared taken aback by the administration’s abrupt action.

media:content.media:title: Army Secretary Daniel Driscoll and acting Army Chief of Staff Christopher LaNeve prepare to testify before the House Armed Services Committee on May 15, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: ‘20 years is enough’: Trump puts a timeline on limiting Iran’s nuclear program
item.description: The allowance for Iran to enrich uranium at any point in the future — even decades out — marks a shift for the president.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump said he isn’t ready to accept a deal with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Alex Gangitano

item.title: Pentagon officials stunned by Hegseth decision on troops in Poland
item.description: It wasn’t clear why the Defense secretary issued the order not to send troops on a routine mission to a country the administration refers to as a “model ally.”

media:content.media:title: Soldiers of the U.S. Army 2nd Cavalry Regiment Stryker Brigade watch Apache helicopters engage during NATO military exercises near Bemowo Piskie, Poland, on May 7, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
